faqs.q: Is substance use disorder a lack of willpower?
faqs.a: No. It isn't a character defect or a moral failing. Repeated use can change brain circuits involved in reward, motivation, and self-control, which is why stopping is genuinely difficult and why it's understood as a health issue.

faqs.q: What is the difference between tolerance and withdrawal?
faqs.a: Tolerance means needing more of the substance to get the same effect, while withdrawal means uncomfortable symptoms when use stops or slows down. Both often show up as part of the condition.

faqs.q: Can substance use disorder be treated?
faqs.a: Yes, recovery is common and effective treatments exist, including counseling, behavioral therapies, medications for certain substances, and peer support. A return to use after stopping is best understood as part of a chronic condition that can be managed, not proof that change is impossible.
